items.project_definition: The unique identifier for the Git feature branch dedicated to the initial implementation of the application.
The specific conformance level of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines requiring the most common accessibility barriers to be removed.
A set of Gherkin-style conditions that must be met to verify a user story is functionally complete.
A classification label from a predefined list (Food, Transport, etc.) used to group spending entries.
The timestamp marking when the technical specification document was first established.
The primary data entity containing financial outlay details including id, amount, date, category, description, and createdAt.
A personal financial utility for recording, listing, and aggregating spending without user authentication.
Abbreviation for Functional Requirements, referencing the specific mandatory system behaviors.
Requirement for the creation of a new spending entry via a form with amount, date, category, and description.
Requirement for input validation ensuring the financial amount is strictly positive and greater than zero.
Requirement for date validation that permits future dates but triggers a warning notification.
Requirement for a restricted selection of spending labels to ensure data consistency.
Requirement for sorting the spending list in descending chronological order.
Requirement for the removal of spending entries subject to a user confirmation prompt.
Requirement for the calculation and display of the cumulative sum of all spending entries.
Requirement for the calculation and display of the total number of spending records.
Requirement to save data into the browser's local key-value store for persistence between page reloads.
Requirement to retrieve previously stored spending data from the browser's local storage upon app initialization.
Requirement to show a specific UI state when no spending records are found in the system.
Requirement for the immediate recalculation of totals following any data modification.
Explicit constraint forbidding the update of existing records in v1; changes require a delete-and-recreate cycle.
Requirement for partial data loading, limiting the initial dashboard view to the 50 most recent records.
The isolated Git development line where the feature implementation occurs before merging into main.
The implicit requirement to handle currency values as decimals with two-point precision to avoid floating-point errors.
A Gherkin keyword describing the initial state or precondition of a test scenario.
The international standardization organization providing the format for date representations.
The specific international standard for representing dates and times as strings (YYYY-MM-DD) used for expense date attributes.
A verification method that can be executed in isolation to validate a specific user story without external dependencies.
The original user-provided description and constraints used as the seed for the project requirements.
The sole mechanism for client-side data persistence in v1, ensuring data survives browser restarts without a backend server.
A RFC 2119-style keyword indicating an absolute requirement that cannot be omitted from the implementation.
An explicit negative constraint used to define out-of-scope functionality for the current version.
The highest priority level assigned to critical core functionalities required for the basic application to be viable.
The medium priority level assigned to value-added features that are not strictly necessary for basic data entry.
Abbreviation for Success Criteria, defining the quantitative and qualitative benchmarks for a successful delivery.
Performance benchmark requiring a new expense to be added in less than 30 seconds from dashboard load.
Performance benchmark requiring immediate rendering of local data without asynchronous loading indicators.
Performance benchmark requiring a deletion and confirmation flow to be completed in under 5 seconds.
Accuracy benchmark ensuring the aggregate total matches the sum of all individual line items.
Persistence benchmark requiring data to remain available across different browser sessions.
Interactivity benchmark requiring the application to become usable within 2 seconds of loading.
Compliance benchmark requiring zero critical accessibility errors according to WCAG AA standards.
The temporal marker for the synchronization session where final scope decisions (like the removal of editing) were made.
The current lifecycle stage of the specification document (currently marked as Draft).
The functional requirement for creating new spending records.
The functional requirement for retrieving and displaying spending history.
The functional requirement for removing erroneous spending records.
The functional requirement for displaying aggregated spending statistics.
A Gherkin keyword describing the expected outcome or post-condition of a test scenario.
The fixed monetary unit used for all financial calculations and displays in v1.
The Web Content Accessibility Guidelines used as the standard to ensure the dashboard is usable by people with disabilities.
A Gherkin keyword describing the specific action or trigger performed by the user.
The browser compatibility target limiting support to the most recent two versions of major web browsers.
The specific limit of records to be rendered initially on the dashboard to optimize performance.
The maximum acceptable time in seconds for a user to successfully add an expense.
The maximum acceptable time in seconds for a user to complete a deletion flow.
The initial version of the project, characterized by limited scope: add/view/delete functionality and local persistence only.
The performance target for application Time-to-Interactive (TTI) on a standard broadband connection.
